search_queries_used: site:dha.gov.ae atopic dermatitis issue 2 pediatric follow-up history red flags
site:nice.org.uk/guidance/cg57 atopic eczema children assessment referral
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic emollient, topical steroid, antimicrobial, dose, duration, referral, or emergency instruction
automatic diagnosis, investigation, treatment, medication, dose, procedure, referral, escalation, or discharge instructions
search snippets, landing pages, and broad source-family matches without an exact applicable section
rejection_reasons: Severity requires a complete clinician assessment and cannot be inferred from generic fields.
No medicine, dose, treatment, referral, or urgency conclusion was generated.
Autonomous clinical output is outside this documentation-only research programme and was not mapped.
Only opened exact official documents and directly applicable sections were accepted as evidence.
population_applicability: Children with clinician-established or suspected atopic dermatitis returning for review; infant, adolescent, infection, allergy, growth, sleep, school, and safeguarding contexts need age-appropriate assessment.
setting_applicability: UAE pediatric, primary, telehealth, and dermatology follow-up with direct examination when needed.
UAE_applicability: The primary source is UAE DHA guidance; pediatric medicine, formulary, infection, allergy, safeguarding, emergency, and referral decisions remain clinician- and pathway-specific.
superseded_check: No newer DHA atopic-dermatitis issue was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact UAE DHA history section supports documenting pediatric eczema interval course, itch, dryness, symptoms, sleep or functional impact, morphology and age-linked distribution without inferring severity or cause.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers itch, dry skin, erythema, vesicles, crusting, scaling, excoriation, lichenification, fissuring, age-linked distribution, atopy, sleep, and quality-of-life impact.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact red-flag section supports documenting rapid worsening, pain, fever, lethargy, blistering, infection, mucosal or systemic concern only when assessed; it does not assert absence or generate escalation.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Lists non-blanching rash, rapidly worsening painful eczema, fever or lethargy, respiratory distress, clustered blisters, punched-out erosions, recurrent infection, pregnancy, and psychosocial concerns.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The management section supports documenting a clinician-entered plan, safety net, arranged follow-up and parent or patient questions without generating skin-care, medicine, dose, referral, or emergency advice.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Covers clinician-led trigger reduction, skin-barrier care, education, and pharmacological choices; no medicine dose is mapped.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: Morphology, age-linked distribution, atopy, dry-skin, differential, and selected investigation context were reviewed; no diagnosis or test is generated.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports morphology and distribution review, atopy history, dry skin, selected non-routine tests, and differential-diagnosis assessment.

unresolved_source_gaps: Age and weight, exact morphology and sites, trigger and exposure history, treatment and adherence, medicine and dose, response and adverse effects, growth, sleep and school impact, infection status, diagnosis, severity, investigations, referral, and follow-up interval remain unsupported.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
